info.name: Restful Booker API — Portfolio Test Suite
info.description:
A comprehensive, portfolio-grade API test collection for the Restful Booker API (https://restful-booker.herokuapp.com).

## Folder Structure
- **00 - Authentication** — Token generation. Run first.
- **01 - Health Check** — Smoke test.
- **02 - GET Bookings** — Listing, filtering, negative cases.
- **03 - POST Create Booking** — Valid creation, validation, security.
- **04 - PUT Full Update** — Full replacement, negative, auth boundary.
- **05 - PATCH Partial Update** — Partial update with persistence verification.
- **06 - DELETE Booking** — Delete with existence verification, negative, security.
- **07 - Integration Flows** — Chained end-to-end scenarios.

## How to Run
1. Import this collection and the accompanying environment file.
2. Select the **Restful Booker - Local** environment.
3. Run **00 - Authentication** first to populate `{{authToken}}`.
4. Run remaining folders in order, or run the full collection via Newman:
   ```
   newman run collection.json -e environment.json --reporters cli,htmlextra
   ```

## Variables Used
All variables are collection-scoped. No hardcoded IDs anywhere. Credentials are environment-scoped.

item.name: 00 - Authentication

request.description: Generates a bearer token using admin credentials stored in the environment. Token is saved to collection variable `authToken` for use in all write operations.

item.description: Authentication endpoints. Run this first — all mutating requests depend on {{authToken}}.

item.name: 01 - Health Check

request.description: Confirms the API is reachable and returns 201 Created. Used as a pre-run smoke test.

item.description: Smoke test. Run before any test suite to confirm the API is up.

item.name: 02 - GET Bookings

exec: pm.test("Response is an array of booking IDs", () => pm.expect(validate(json)).to.be.true);
pm.test("At least one booking exists", () => pm.expect(json.length).to.be.above(0));
// Save first booking ID for downstream tests

request.description: Returns all booking IDs. Validates list structure and confirms at least one record exists.

item.name: GET - Retrieve Booking by Valid ID

exec: pm.test("Booking detail schema is valid", () => pm.expect(validate(json)).to.be.true);

request.description: Fetches a specific booking by ID (set by the Get All Bookings request). Validates full schema and field types.

item.name: GET - Retrieve Booking by Invalid ID (Negative)

request.description: Expects 404 when querying a booking ID of 0, which is guaranteed to be non-existent.
Filters bookings by a known first name. Validates results are returned and IDs are integers.

exec: pm.test("Filter by lastname returns an array", () => pm.expect(pm.response.json()).to.be.an("array"));

request.description: Filters bookings by a known last name.

item.name: GET - Filter by First Name + Last Name (Valid)

exec: pm.test("Combined name filter returns an array", () => pm.expect(pm.response.json()).to.be.an("array"));

request.description: Filters bookings with both name params. Verifies the combined filter returns results.

item.name: GET - Filter by First Name Only, Invalid Last Name (Negative)

exec: pm.test("No results for non-matching name combination", () => {

request.description: Expects an empty array when no record matches the combined filter.

item.name: GET - Filter by Check-in and Check-out Date (Valid)

request.description: Filters by date range. Validates response is an array.

item.name: GET - Filter by Malformed Date Format (Negative)

exec: pm.test("Malformed date returns 400 or 200 with empty result", () => {

request.description: Sends non-ISO date strings to verify the API rejects or handles them gracefully.

item.description: All GET /booking scenarios — list, filter by name, filter by date, and edge cases.

item.name: 03 - POST Create Booking

request.description: Creates a new booking with all required fields. Validates response schema, saves bookingid to {{createdBookingId}} for downstream use.

item.name: POST - Create Booking with Empty Fields (Negative)

request.description: Sends all empty strings. Expects API to return a 4xx validation error.

item.name: POST - Create Booking with Whitespace-Only Fields (Negative)

request.description: Sends whitespace-only strings for name fields. Expects API to reject as invalid input.

item.name: POST - Create Booking Without Auth Token (Security)

exec: // This test documents the current API behaviour.
// In a production system you would assert 401 here.
pm.test("POST /booking without auth - response is 200 (API allows unauthenticated creation)", () => {

request.description: Intentionally omits the auth cookie. Note: restful-booker allows unauthenticated booking creation — this test documents that behaviour explicitly.

item.description: POST /booking — valid creation, negative validation, and security boundary tests.

item.name: 04 - PUT Full Update Booking

exec: pm.test("Response schema is valid after PUT", () => pm.expect(validate(json)).to.be.true);

request.description: Performs a full replacement of the booking created in folder 03. Validates all fields are updated in the response.

item.name: PUT - Full Update with Empty Fields (Negative)

request.description: Sends invalid data in a PUT request. Expects 4xx validation error.

item.name: PUT - Full Update Without Auth Token (Security)

request.description: Attempts a PUT without auth cookie. Expects 403 Forbidden.

item.description: PUT /booking/:id — full replacement with valid data, negative cases, and auth boundary.

item.name: 05 - PATCH Partial Update Booking
PATCH - Update First Name and Last Name Only (Valid)

exec: pm.test("totalprice still present after partial update", () => {

request.description: Partially updates only firstname and lastname on the booking. Verifies other fields are unchanged.

item.name: PATCH - Verify Partial Update Persisted (GET After PATCH)

exec: pm.test("Patched firstname persisted in GET response", () => {
pm.test("Patched lastname persisted in GET response", () => {

request.description: Fetches the booking after PATCH to confirm the partial update persisted in storage.
Attempts a PATCH without auth cookie. Expects 403 Forbidden.

item.description: PATCH /booking/:id — partial update and persistence verification.

item.name: 06 - DELETE Booking
DELETE - Delete Booking by Valid ID

request.description: Deletes the booking created and updated in previous folders. Expects 201 Created (restful-booker convention).

item.name: GET - Verify Booking No Longer Exists After DELETE

request.description: Confirms the deleted booking returns 404. This is the critical assertion that proves the DELETE worked.
Attempts to delete a booking ID that cannot exist. Expects 404 or 405.
Attempts DELETE without auth cookie. Expects 403 Forbidden.

item.description: DELETE /booking/:id — successful deletion, existence verification, negative, and security cases.

item.name: 07 - Integration Flows
Flow 01 - Create → Update → Verify

request.description: Integration step 1: Create a booking. Saves bookingid to {{int1_bookingId}}.
Integration step 2: Partially update the booking name.
Integration step 3: Fetch booking and verify the update persisted end-to-end.
Integration step 4: Clean up the test booking to avoid polluting the dataset.

item.description: End-to-end flow: Create a booking → patch name → GET to verify → DELETE to clean up.

item.name: Flow 02 - Create → Delete → Verify Deletion

request.description: Integration step 1: Create a booking specifically for deletion testing.
Integration step 2: Delete the created booking.

item.name: Step 3 - GET Verify Booking is Gone

request.description: Integration step 3: Confirm the booking no longer exists after deletion.

item.description: End-to-end flow: Create a booking → DELETE it → confirm 404 on GET.

item.name: Flow 03 - Get Existing → Update → Verify
Step 1 - GET All Bookings (Extract First ID)

exec: pm.test("Extract first booking ID from list", () => {

request.description: Integration step 1: Fetch all bookings and extract the first ID for use in subsequent steps.
Integration step 2: Patch an existing (not test-created) booking.
Integration step 3: Verify the patch persisted end-to-end via a fresh GET.

item.description: End-to-end flow: Extract a real booking from the API → patch it → verify via GET.
End-to-end chained scenarios. Each flow is self-contained and uses collection variables to pass state between steps. Run folder 00 (Auth) before executing these flows.
